page: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 server family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, supported by 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 2835 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong computational capabilities, with single-core stress-ng performance and Geekbench multi-core scores reaching the top 10% tier. Multi-core stress-ng performance, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench single-core results fall within the top 25% tier. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth is strong, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50% average tier. Cryptographic and integer operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-level instruction sets such as AVX-512, AES, and SHA-NI.

The D48ads_v6 exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs when executing language model workloads. While prompt processing and text generation for small 135M models rank in the top 25%, text generation for large 70B models drops to the bottom 10% due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. However, the instance excels in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, both scoring in the top 25%. Given its high-capacity local storage, balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities, this server is highly suited for relational databases, software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 2835 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers top-10% performance in single-core stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench benchmarks. While memory bandwidth for uncached large blocks is average, its cached small-block bandwidth and database operations rank in the top 25%. The lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale LLM text generation, which falls into the bottom 10%. However, with strong multi-threaded decompression and high-capacity local storage, the D48ads_v6 is highly suited for database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 2835 GB local disk. Powered by an AMD EPYC CPU, it delivers top-tier single-core and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, optimized for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity local storage, and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB RAM.
Includes 2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Delivers top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench benchmarks.
Strong database operations and Redis SET performance, ranking in the top 25%.
Tradeoffs include poor large-model LLM text generation due to the absence of GPU acceleration.
Best suited for relational databases, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
